Name: curvature_aware_quantization
Title: Curvature-Aware Adaptive Mixed Precision Quantization Framework
Short Hypothesis: Can we develop a curvature-aware adaptive mixed precision quantization framework that uses geometrical insights to optimize quantization strategies, improving both efficiency and accuracy without extensive retraining?
Related Work: While existing works like EMQ and InfoQ focus on training-free proxies and layer importance for mixed-precision quantization, they often overlook the underlying geometric properties of the loss landscape. Our approach distinguishes itself by leveraging curvature information to inform quantization decisions, potentially leading to more effective bit-width allocations that maintain model integrity during compression.
Abstract: In this proposal, we explore a novel curvature-aware adaptive mixed precision quantization framework that integrates geometric insights from the loss landscape to optimize quantization strategies. By employing curvature diagnostics, we aim to understand how the quantization of weights and activations impacts the model's local geometry and overall accuracy. This framework will utilize projection-based mechanistic probes to assess the curvature of the loss surface at various layers, enabling targeted quantization decisions that preserve critical model features while reducing computational overhead. We will validate our hypotheses through empirical experimentation, comparing our curvature-aware approach against existing training-free quantization methods. We aim to demonstrate that our method not only achieves superior accuracy but also enhances the efficiency of mixed precision deployment in real-world applications. This research has the potential to provide a deeper understanding of the interaction between quantization and model performance, contributing to more robust and deployable neural network architectures.
Experiments: 1. Conduct a series of experiments on standard datasets (e.g., ImageNet) with various architectures (ResNet, MobileNet) to benchmark the performance of our curvature-aware quantization framework against existing methods (e.g., EMQ, InfoQ). 2. Implement curvature diagnostics using second-derivative information of the loss landscape to inform bit-width decisions for each layer. 3. Evaluate model performance based on accuracy and computational efficiency using metrics such as Top-1 accuracy and FLOPs. 4. Perform ablation studies to isolate the effects of curvature-awareness on quantization performance. 5. Use multiple seeds to assess variance and reproducibility of results.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the difficulty in accurately estimating curvature in high-dimensional spaces, which may limit the effectiveness of our approach. Additionally, the reliance on geometric properties may not generalize well across all architectures or tasks. The experiments may also face challenges in reproducibility due to variance in training data and model initialization.
